The pinnacle attracts large pelagics like reef sharks and occasional manta rays. Best dived from November to April.

name: Wang Long
region: Asia, Thailand, Southern Thailand, Krabi, Koh Phi Phi
ocean: Indian Ocean, Andaman Sea, Strait Of Malacca
description: Wang Long offers vibrant coral gardens teeming with marine life, including clownfish, moray eels, and occasional reef sharks. The site features gentle slopes and rocky outcrops, ideal for relaxed exploration. Visibility is typically clear, with warm waters year-round. Soft corals and sea fans sway in mild currents, creating a colorful underwater landscape. Suitable for all skill levels, it's a great spot for both beginners and experienced divers. The area is part of the stunning Phi Phi archipelago, known for its rich biodiversity.

name: Tong Cave
region: Asia, Thailand, Southern Thailand, Krabi, Koh Phi Phi
ocean: Indian Ocean, Andaman Sea, Strait Of Malacca
description: Descend into a stunning limestone cave system off Koh Phi Phi, where shafts of sunlight pierce the water, creating dramatic light effects. Swim through narrow passages and open chambers teeming with glassfish and occasional blacktip reef sharks. The cave's entrance sits at 12 meters, with depths reaching 18 meters inside. Strong currents outside make this dive best suited for experienced divers. Expect excellent visibility and a mix of soft corals and sponges coating the walls. Night dives reveal bioluminescent plankton.

name: The Rock
region: Asia, Thailand, Southern Thailand, Phuket, Racha Noi Island
ocean: Indian Ocean, Andaman Sea, Strait Of Malacca
description: A stunning dive site off Racha Noi Island, known for its dramatic granite boulders and vibrant marine life. The clear waters offer excellent visibility, often exceeding 20 meters. Schools of barracuda, trevally, and occasional reef sharks patrol the area. Soft corals and sea fans cover the rocks, attracting colorful reef fish. Currents can be strong, making it ideal for drift diving. Depths range from 5 to 30 meters, suitable for various skill levels. The site is a favorite for underwater photographers due to its rich biodiversity and striking topography.

name: Atlantis Wreck
region: Asia, Thailand, Southern Thailand, Phang Nga, Similan Islands
ocean: Indian Ocean, Andaman Sea
description: This wreck dive in the Similan Islands offers a thrilling exploration of a sunken cargo ship resting at 30 meters. The structure is encrusted with colorful corals and sponges, attracting schools of batfish, barracuda, and occasional reef sharks. Strong currents make this an advanced dive, but visibility often exceeds 20 meters. The intact hull and cargo holds provide exciting swim-throughs, while the surrounding sandy slope hosts garden eels and stingrays. Best visited during the November to April season when conditions are calm.

name: Shark Fin Reef
region: Asia, Thailand, Southern Thailand, Phang Nga, Similan Islands, Payang Island
ocean: Indian Ocean, Andaman Sea
description: A stunning reef dive in the Similan Islands, known for its dramatic rock formations resembling a shark fin. The site features vibrant coral gardens teeming with reef fish, moray eels, and occasional leopard sharks. Strong currents attract larger pelagics like manta rays and whale sharks. Depths range from 5 to 30 meters, offering excellent visibility. Best suited for experienced divers due to the currents. The underwater topography includes swim-throughs and overhangs, creating an exciting dive.

name: Nai Harn
region: Asia, Thailand, Southern Thailand, Phuket
ocean: Indian Ocean, Andaman Sea, Strait Of Malacca
description: Nai Harn offers clear waters with visibility often exceeding 20 meters. The site features a mix of coral-covered boulders and sandy patches, home to reef fish, moray eels, and occasional leopard sharks. Gentle currents make it ideal for beginners, while deeper sections attract experienced divers. The sloping reef transitions to a sandy bottom at 18 meters, where garden eels sway in the current. Night dives reveal octopus and crustaceans. Calm conditions prevail from November to April, with warm water year-round. Nearby pinnacles host schools of barracuda and trevally.

region: Asia, Thailand, Southern Thailand, Phang Nga, Similan Islands
ocean: Indian Ocean, Andaman Sea
description: This sheltered bay in the Similan Islands offers calm conditions, ideal for beginners. Vibrant coral gardens teem with reef fish, moray eels, and occasional leopard sharks. The sloping reef transitions to sandy patches where garden eels sway. Clear waters and gentle currents make it perfect for relaxed exploration. Depth ranges from 5 to 20 meters, with boulders creating swim-throughs. Night dives reveal octopus and crustaceans. The bay's protection from strong currents ensures year-round diving opportunities.

name: Nongasem
region: Asia, Thailand, Southern Thailand, Krabi, Koh Phi Phi
ocean: Indian Ocean, Andaman Sea, Strait Of Malacca
description: A vibrant reef dive with soft corals and sea fans swaying in mild currents. Expect schools of batfish, barracuda, and occasional reef sharks patrolling the slopes. The site features a mix of boulders and sandy patches, with swim-throughs adding excitement. Visibility is typically clear, around 15-20 meters. Ideal for intermediate divers, with depths ranging 10-25 meters. Night dives reveal octopus and crustaceans. Located near Koh Phi Phi, easily accessible by boat.
